title: stenophyllus capillaris f. asperulus
intro: Stenophyllus capillaris f. asperulus, also known as the lesser club moss or Lycopodium clavatum, is a type of lycopodiaceous plant that belongs to the Stenophyllus genus. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antitumor activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and potentially acting as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent. Stenophyllus capillaris f. asperulus can be prepared into herbal remedies such as tea, tinctures, and infusions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
